feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: California hikers hit with suspected norovirus outbreak
entry.summary: One hiker had to be airlifted from the trail for medical care.

entry.title: One Weight Loss Strategy Is 5x More Effective Than Ozempic, Study Shows
entry.summary: Ozempic and other semaglutide medications have been working wonders in terms of shedding pounds for many people.

entry.title: αKG-mediated carnitine synthesis drives DNA repair via histone acetylation
entry.summary: The metabolite αKG promotes carnitine synthesis and increases site-specific histone acetylation, thereby promoting homologous recombination-mediated DNA repair, which has potential implications for chemoresistant cancers.

entry.title: After years of long COVID, Seattle father and former teacher turns to UW Medicine for help
entry.summary: Jesse Hagopian meets a lot of people through a computer screen these days. The former Garfield High School teacher, musician, coach and father of two is one of

entry.title: Blood testing is now included in screening recommendations for colon and rectal cancer
entry.summary: The American Cancer Society is adding some new testing options to its screening guideline for colorectal cancers – and for the first time, that includes a blood test.

entry.title: My Patient Invited Me To Her Home To Watch Her Die. What I Saw When I Arrived Gave Me Goosebumps.
entry.summary: “She sat down and paused for a few seconds, and then looked at me earnestly and asked, ‘Will you be there to hold my hand?’”

entry.title: Human haematopoietic stem cells remember inflammatory stress
entry.summary: Using xenograft inflammation–recovery models and single-cell multiomics, a haematopoietic stem cell population after inflammatory stress is identified.

entry.title: Universal transcriptomic hallmarks of mammalian ageing and mortality
entry.summary: Integration of gene expression data from multiple tissues across four mammalian species reveals conserved transcriptomic signatures of mammalian ageing and mortality and uncovers the modular architecture of ageing and mortality hallmarks.

entry.title: State Medical Board: Physician Assistant Failed To Meet Standards Of Care
entry.summary: RALEIGH, N.C. — A physician assistant has been reprimanded by the North Carolina Medical Board following an investigation into prescribing practices, patient monitoring failures, and inadequate medical documentation involving multiple patients, including one …

entry.title: Cluster of mpox cases identified in Wisconsin; prevention resources
entry.summary: The Wisconsin Department of Health Services (DHS) encourages Wisconsinites to take steps to prevent mpox after cases were recently confirmed in southeastern and northern Wisconsin.

entry.title: Woman, 42, Suffers Burst Brain Aneurysm and Stroke While Driving. It Started With 2 Sudden Symptoms
entry.summary: Nichole Vallette, 42, had a sudden, severe headache and nausea while driving to work. It was the first sign of a life-threatening ruptured brain aneurysm and stroke.

entry.title: Sex advice: I haven't told my wife about why my last marriage ended. It feels like I'm lying to her.
entry.summary: She knows everything about me ... except this.

entry.title: More Than This Many Hours of Sleep Is Linked to Early Death, Scientists Find
entry.summary: New research found that there's such a thing as too much sleep, which can accelerated aging and a higher risk of mortality.

entry.title: This 1 Activity Could Actually Slow Down Aging. You Might Already Be Doing It Without Realizing
entry.summary: Unlike chronological age, which simply refers to how long you’ve been alive, biological age refers to how fast your cells, tissues, and organs are aging.

entry.title: A Disease Once Confined to the Tropics Just Saw a 359% Surge in the US
entry.summary: Cases of the viral disease skyrocketed in 2024, new CDC data shows.

entry.title: Europe beefs up Ebola detection as Congo epidemic surges
entry.summary: The bloc isn’t taking any risks as the virus spreads faster than health workers can contain it.

entry.title: Doctors Say These Are the 10 Signs You’re Not Eating Enough Protein
entry.summary: Nutrition experts share the low protein symptoms that can help you tell if you're not getting enough of this key nutrient. Plus, how much to eat per meal.

entry.title: They had major medical crises. Doctors called them panic attacks
entry.summary: In the ER, Christy Kirk was told her symptoms were anxiety. They weren’t – she had a hole in her heart that had gone undetected, leading to a stroke.

entry.title: Why You Gain Weight During Menopause—and What to Do About It
entry.summary: Learn why you gain weight during menopause and get expert-backed tips on managing weight, improving nutrition and boosting activity during this life stage.

entry.title: Ebola Cases Top 1,000 As Outbreak Rages In Congo (Live Updates)
entry.summary: More than 220 people have died and there's no sign the spread is slowing.

entry.title: Ebola, chemical plants and health, hantavirus, common colds, heat and more
entry.summary: The Dose: May 27

entry.title: The #1 Breakfast to Eat to Support Your Metabolism, According to Dietitians
entry.summary: Spinach & Egg Scramble with Raspberries is an easy and nutritious start to your day. Dietitians explain why it supports your metabolism.

entry.title: Hantavirus cases from cruise outbreak rise to 13 following new case in Spain, WHO says
entry.summary: GENEVA — The number of cases of Hantavirus linked to a cruise ship at the centre of an outbreak has increased to 13, the head of the World Health Organization said on Wednesday.“Spain reported a new case among the passengers who are in quarantine, which bring…

entry.title: Northern California is enduring a 'late-spring surge' of rotavirus
entry.summary: Rotavirus can cause severe watery diarrhea and vomiting, especially among small children between the ages of 3 months to 3 years.

entry.title: 8 rumors involving disease-spreading ticks we've investigated
entry.summary: As climate change allows the blood-sucking arachnids to proliferate, so do tick-borne diseases and related conspiracy theories.

entry.title: A Strange Pattern Could Explain The Mysterious Origins of Brain Cancer
entry.summary: Not every region of your brain is equally susceptible to cancer.

entry.title: Blood testing is now included in screening recommendations for colon and rectal cancer
entry.summary: The American Cancer Society is adding some new testing options to its screening guideline for colorectal cancers – and for the first time, that includes a blood test.

entry.title: 5 ways daily cannabis use can affect your body and mind
entry.summary: Proponents say the drug can help with sleep and pain, but there’s little research surrounding potential long-term effects of daily use.

entry.title: ‘Complicated’ circumstances test public health playbook for World Cup preparations
entry.summary: Although rare diseases like Ebola are concerning, experts say that most public health preparation for the World Cup has been focused on familiar issues – but ramped up to match the scale of the event.

entry.title: Diagnostic dilemma: Whiplike rashes appeared on a woman's back after she ate shiitake mushrooms
entry.summary: A woman developed bright-red rashes on her back that looked like marks from a whip, and at first, it wasn't clear what caused them.

entry.title: Donald Trump Administration Blocks Disease Experts From WHO Ebola Talks — Report
entry.summary: President Donald Trump‘s administration has barred top federal health experts from joining World Health Organization (WHO) discussions on Ebola. The United...

entry.title: Why mosquitoes prefer to bite some people over others
entry.summary: The Mosquito Control and Research Unit is ramping up efforts to keep mosquito populations under control.

entry.title: Scientists create supercharged vitamin K that helps the brain heal itself
entry.summary: Scientists in Japan have created powerful new vitamin K-based compounds that may help the brain regenerate lost neurons — a breakthrough that could one day change how diseases like Alzheimer’s and Parkinson’s are treated. By combining vitamin K with component…

entry.title: Are Your Hobbies Reducing Your Dementia Risk? 4 Questions to Ask Yourself
entry.summary: Dementia is now the leading cause of death in Australia.

entry.title: Bangladesh measles cases: Hundreds of children die within months
entry.summary: The country has recorded more than 60,000 suspected cases of measles in just over two months.

entry.title: Pacific Crest Trail hikers hit by sudden stomach illness in California
entry.summary: Pacific Crest Trail hikers have reported gastrointestinal illness in California, but county health officials have not publicly confirmed a broader outbreak

entry.title: Finland has spent decades building a tunnel 430 metres into 1.9-billion-year-old bedrock to store nuclear waste for 100,000 years — and the current plan is to seal it, leave no marker, and hope no future civilisation ever finds it
entry.summary: On Olkiluoto Island, off Finland’s west coast, a tunnel runs 430 metres down into bedrock that has sat largely undisturbed for about 1.9 billion years. The facility is called Onkalo. When it is full, the plan is to seal it, restore the surface, and leave noth…

entry.title: Ebola disease outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda
entry.summary: On 17 May 2026, the World Health Organization (WHO) declared the Ebola virus disease outbreak caused by Bundibugyo virus in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda a Public Health Emergency of International Concern.

entry.title: 20 foods that support brain health and function
entry.summary: The research on nutrition and brain health is more nuanced than most headlines suggest, and these 20 foods have the strongest evidence behind them

entry.title: Elsevier retracts study tying sudden infant death syndrome to vaccinations
entry.summary: Elsevier has retracted a 2021 study claiming sudden infant death syndrome is linked to vaccines over concerns the paper might influence patient care. The single-author study, by longtim…

entry.title: Golden Retriever Rushes To Help After Man Falls off Paddle Board
entry.summary: A Golden Retriever named Gus bravely took on the role of a lifeguard as he rushed to help a man in this adorable Instagram video.

entry.title: I Took Vitamin D Supplements Every Day for a Week, and This Is What Happened to My Body
entry.summary: A dietitian reveals what happens when you take vitamin D every day. Learn how daily supplements support bone and immune health if you have a deficiency.

entry.title: 8 Healthy Dopamine-Boosting Foods, According to Experts
entry.summary: Food plays an important part in maintaining healthy dopamine levels. Here’s what to eat—and avoid—to support your brain’s dopamine production.

entry.title: Vitamin D analog shuts down pancreatic cancer's shield in a clinical trial
entry.summary: A small clinical trial led by Dana-Farber Cancer Institute researchers has put a Salk Institute idea to the test in patients: that activating the vitamin D receptor can help reshape the protective environment surrounding pancreatic tumors in ways that could m…

entry.title: Ozempic Is Finally Turning the Tide Against Obesity, New Data Shows
entry.summary: As the rate of GLP-1 prescriptions in the U.S. has risen, the obesity rate has dropped.

entry.title: Ebola Outbreak At Risk Of Becoming ‘Deadliest On Record,’ Humanitarian Group Warns (Live Updates)
entry.summary: More than 220 people have died and there's no sign the spread is slowing.

entry.title: Scientists at Stanford Find a Way To Regrow Aging Knee Cartilage and Prevent Arthritis
entry.summary: Millions of people suffer from cartilage loss and arthritis. This could be a breakthrough to relieve a lot of pain and suffering.

entry.title: Hidden brain circuit could explain how movement errors sharpen new skills
entry.summary: While humans are acquiring new skills that entail performing coordinated movements, such as walking, playing an instrument or skateboarding, their brains are known to continuously detect mistakes and correct movements over time. This gradual acquisition of ta…

entry.title: Five-tea comparison reveals kombucha's biological properties depend on starting point
entry.summary: Over the past few years, kombucha has become one of the world's most popular fermented beverages. While most consumers focus primarily on its taste, scientists are increasingly analyzing its chemical composition and potential biological properties.

entry.title: Launching a Second Scientific Revolution
entry.summary: If we continue as we have been, the prospect of our children living shorter, less healthy lives than we do is a real possibility.
